journal_entry.reason: Manufacturing overhead costs for factory production staff meals are product costs that must be capitalized to work-in-progress inventory under IFRS. The corporate credit card payment creates a liability. The $125 is added to inventory carrying amount and will be expensed as cost of sales when the manufactured goods are sold.

output_debit_classifier.reason: The transaction involves a meal purchase for factory production staff classified as manufacturing overhead. Under IFRS, manufacturing overhead is a product cost that must be capitalized to inventory (IAS 2), not expensed immediately. The meal cost increases work-in-progress inventory by $125. The corporate credit card payment creates a liability (credit card payable) of $125. No tax is mentioned in the transaction, so no tax lines are included. Total debits ($125) equal total credits ($125).
